rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ARJUN ARORA RECOVABLE TRUST DATED JANUARY 19 2022
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Property To Be Titled In Name Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/2023. This tentative ruling is issued by the judicial officer. Petitioner's Probate Code Section 850 Petition, among other things, seeks an order declaring decedent's gametic material an asset of the Trust. Petitioner must file an amendment to the petition, including a memorandum of points and authorities, addressing the factual and legal issues raised by a request for a posthumous transfer of gametic material. Broadly, Petitioner must allege the purpose for which transfer is sought and identify the specific evidence that decedent intended for posthumous use of his gametic material for that purpose. Assuming the purpose is posthumous conception, Petitioner must allege facts sufficient to overcome the presumption that did not intend his gametic material to be used for posthumous conception. (See Robertson v. Saadat (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 630, 647.) Petitioner must also make an affirmative showing that decedent intended: (1) the Trust to have ownership over his gametic material for the intended purpose after his death (recognizing that gametic material is a unique type of property not governed by general laws regarding gifts or transfers upon death); (2) the Trustee to control the disposition of his gametic material upon his death; and, (3) the Trustee to authorize the use of his gametic material for posthumous conception, if this is the intended purpose. (See id. at pp. 640-648.) If decedent's intent is not evidence by a writing or writings, Petitioner should address why a writing should not be required, as it is in the heirship context under Probate Code section 249.5. (See id. at p. 647 [reserving decision whether intent must be evidenced by writing].) The amendment must include a copy of the sperm storage agreement or explain why it is not provided. The amendment must be properly noticed and the papers properly served, with proofs of notice and service to all parties entitled to notice of an estate proceeding and all parties against whom relief is sought. Amendment to be filed, and a filed stamped copy provided, by 1/23/23.
